questions.question: ‘Infinity Bridge’ is the iconic architectural structure located in which country?
questions.explanation: The ‘Infinity Bridge’ in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, has been opened to traffic for the first time recently. The new bridge which is built over the Dubai Creek, beside the Al Shindagha Tunnel, which allowed vehicles to cross the Creek underwater. The Infinity Bridge is part of the Al Shindagha Corridor Project, which was first proposed in 2018.

questions.question: Who are the beneficiaries of the ‘KCR Kit Scheme’ of Telangana?
questions.explanation: The state government launched ‘KCR Kit Scheme’ for pregnant women, to provide all necessary items for pregnant women and the new-born baby. The pregnant women will be provided with financial assistance of Rs. 12,000 in three phases. In case of a baby girl, an additional Rs. 1000 will be given. Recently, KCR Kit is extended to lactating mothers. After the implementation of the scheme, institutional deliveries increased by 22% in the State.

questions.question: As per the UNCTAD Investment Trends Monitor, FDI flows to India …………….. in 2021, than that of 2020.
questions.explanation: The UN Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD) Investment Trends Monitor says that Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) flows to India in 2021 were 26 per cent lower than that of the previous year. The global foreign direct investment flows showed a strong bounce in 2021, increasing 77 per cent to USD 1.65 trillion, from USD 929 billion in 2020. China saw a record 20 per cent increase with USD 179 billion of inflows, driven by strong services FDI.

questions.question: Who is the first Indian woman tennis player to rise to the top spot in either singles or doubles?
questions.explanation: Sania Mirza was the Indian woman tennis player to rise to the top spot in either singles or doubles, when she claimed the No 1 place in Doubles in 2015. She also holds the record of the first Indian woman to lift a Grand slam doubles title, when she won at the Wimbledon. After a break, she created a record by winning the Fed Cup heart Award. Recently, the ace tennis player has announced retirement after 2022 season.

questions.question: Which state governments signed agreement to rejuvenate the Saraswati River by constructing Adi Badra Dam?
questions.explanation: The Governments of Haryana and Himachal Pradesh entered into an agreement to build a dam at Adi Badri in Yamunanagar district. Along with this, the area will also develop as a pilgrimage site. The objective of the project is to revive the Saraswati River as well as increase ground water level. Adi Badri dam will be built on 31.66 hectares of land in Himachal Pradesh and Rs 215.33 crore will be spent for the same.

questions.question: IES, which was recommended by TSR Subramanian Committee, stands for ………
questions.explanation: The high-level committee was constituted in 2014 under the chairmanship of former Cabinet Secretary TSR Subramanian by the Ministry of Environment, forests and climate change (MoEF & CC) to review environmental laws in the country. The Supreme Court had asked the Centre whether it was planning a dedicated Indian Environment Service in the national bureaucratic set-up, as recommended by the Subramanian Committee.

questions.question: What is the theme of the “The International Day of Education” 2021?

options.label: D
options.text: Equitable and Quality education for all

questions.explanation: “The International Day of Education” is annually celebrated on January 24. This year the theme of the fourth year of celebration is ‘Changing Course, Transforming Education’. The United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) in 2018 proclaimed the celebration of this day. India celebrates ‘National Education day’ celebrated on November 11 to mark the birth anniversary of India’s first Education Minister Maulana Abul Kalam Azad.

questions.question: Which Ministry is associated with the “Online Storage Management (OSM)”?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Agriculture and Family Welfare

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution

questions.explanation: The Department of Food and Public Distribution under Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution with its principal agency, FCI (Food Corporation of India) has developed a roadmap to launch the Online Storage Management (OSM) in the country. The OSM seeks to create a single source of information for the food grains stored in the country for central pool through integrating State portals with the central portal.

questions.question: Which Indian movie won the Best Film award at Dhaka International Film Festival?

options.label: C
options.text: Marakkar: Lion of the Arabian Sea

questions.explanation: ‘Koozhangal’ from India won the best film award in the Asian Film Competition section at the 20th Dhaka International Film Festival. It was directed by P S Vinothraj from Tamil Nadu. Best Actor Award went to Jayasurya for Ranjith Sankar-directed film Sunny. The Best Script Writer Award went to Indranil Roychowdhury and Sugata Sinha for India-Bangladesh film Mayar Jonjal. The special audience award was given to Aimee Baruah Directed film Semkhor. India won 4 out of 17 awards.

questions.question: Buddhadeb Bhattacharjee, who refused the Padma Bhushan award, was the Chief Minister of which state?
questions.explanation: The 77 year-old Communist Party of India (Marxist) leader Buddhadeb Bhattacharjee refused the Padma Bhushan award announced in 2022. He served as the Chief Minister of West Bengal from 2000 to 2011. Legendary singer Sandhya Mukherjee and tabla exponent Anindya Chatterjee also refused the awards. Sandhya Mukherjee has a 70 year long career and has sung thousands of songs for Indian cinema.
